directions.name: Human idea 10 Stage: keep the correct hot kernel, but specialize the fixed 452-tile loop into explicit prologue / steady-state / epilogue control
directions.hypothesis: Round 5 proved that changing only the internal 64x64 tile order on the correct branch is too low-signal: overall runtime improved by only about 0.024 ms and the hot-band kernel itself actually moved the wrong way to about 41.11 us. That says the current bottleneck is not a tiny per-fragment order tweak. The better next move is the user's fixed-shape Stage idea: keep the correct 256x128 / 64x64 kernel and the same layout, but rewrite the 452-tile hot loop so most iterations execute in a simpler steady-state form with fewer generic branch checks and cleaner cp.async handoff. This directly targets hot-loop orchestration tax while preserving correctness and shared/register budgets.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Generic steady-state loop control and stage-transition overhead in the true hot-band kernel rather than operand layout.
directions.risk: The upside is moderate. If the actual wall is still register-limited feed rather than control overhead, this can become a refactor with little runtime impact.
directions.stop_condition: Reject if hot-band runtime and tensor/barrier metrics remain essentially flat after the control specialization.

directions.name: Human idea 9 Ps2r: add a one-fragment shared-to-register lookahead inside the correct full-width sweep
directions.hypothesis: If the loop still wants a feed-path change on the correct branch, the next aggressive option is shared-to-register lookahead inside the full-width 64x64 sweep. Keep the same tiling and shared layout, but overlap the next B-fragment load with the current MMA issue chain. This is a legitimate Ps2r direction, but it is riskier than dir_01 because the correct branch is already at 167 registers/thread.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Warp-local shared-to-register delivery latency inside the full-width hot-band sweep.
directions.risk: High register risk on an already register-limited branch. This can easily worsen occupancy with little benefit.
directions.stop_condition: Reject if registers climb or occupancy worsens with no clear hot-band runtime improvement.

directions.name: Human idea 5 bank-conflict follow-through: keep the correct branch and retune the warp-local B consumer again, but on a different pair schedule
directions.hypothesis: If the loop wants to stay on the Bank Conflict family despite round 5's weak result, the only reasonable version is another bounded consumer-schedule retune with the same shared footprint and no CTA repack. That is lower-risk than Ps2r, but it is also lower-ceiling after round 5 already showed minimal signal for the first order reshuffle.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Warp-local B-fragment order and bank behavior on the correct hot-band kernel.
directions.risk: Round 5 already spent budget on this family and got almost no signal. Another small schedule variant can easily be pure noise.
directions.stop_condition: Reject if the hot-band runtime stays flat again. After that, this family is exhausted on the correct branch.

notes: Round 6 human-idea audit against the latest correct-branch evidence: Tiling stays accepted and fixed at 256x128 / 64x64; Coalescing Access, Data Reuse, Async Copy, and Pg2s stay accepted as baseline infrastructure already present in the kernel; Bank Conflict is still a valid correct-branch family, but round 5 showed that a first issue-order retune had only tiny overall signal and no hot-band win, so it is no longer the top choice; L2 Cache remains deferred because the branch is not L2-bound; Register Reuse is accepted only in the narrow internal-order sense on the correct branch, not as a return to half-panel; Ps2r remains a valid but higher-risk family because the branch is already at 167 regs/thread; Stage becomes the primary idea this round because the shape is fixed, the current loop still carries generic control, and the lower-risk layout/order experiments have not moved the true hotspot enough. Recommended direction dir_01 therefore specializes the fixed 452-tile hot loop before spending more rounds on higher-register feed tricks.
